item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dialogue here, especially in the second log, leans far too hard on overly cryptic near-gibberish without having sufficient imagery or raw content for there to be any discernible method to the madness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, but the dialogue doesn't quite push my creep buttons the way I'd hoped it would. +-0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I'm glad you enjoyed it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah okay, I see what you mean. The implication is that the -2 was indeed too quick in it's movement for anyone to physically stop it in it's tracks. I see now why that isn't so clear. I'll make a revision some time today to tidy that up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, see, the fact that they didn't shoot does make sense. What I can't parse is why they didn't do anything at all. Research and presumably security personnel were both there with her, but not a single person ran over to put hands on the SCP-3389-2 to punch it in what's left of its face? None of them tried to grab onto the doctor to pull her free? I mean, guns aren't their only option here, right?

If the -2 fended them off with unnatural strength or dragged her away too quickly for any of them to catch up, that would make sense. But from the looks of it, all these people just stood there with their fingers up their noses and watched it drag her off. I would readily upvote this if at least a single researcher had the presence of mind to run up to the monster, thwack it on the forehead with a ruler, and say "NO! Bad dog!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m glad you got this to the mainlist! For real, I think this is a fun (if not frightening) idea, and I’m enjoying the final product. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for pointing that out! I originally had a clear explanation for that, but it must've gotten removed by mistake while I was making revisions. I made a small change clarifying that they don't fire because Albright specifically orders them not to, even after she's been grabbed.

As for why they're on the same side of the fence, Albright wanted to be. She wanted to interact with these entities closely, and felt confident in security to keep her safe. Obviously, that's not how it goes, but the point still stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really really liked this up until the point where the guards did absolutely nothing to save the doctor. I can get that they wouldn't want to shoot in case they hit her on accident, but why didn't they try to restrain the -2 instance or grab onto the doctor themselves or otherwise try to separate them physically? For that matter, why did they get on the same side of the fence with the -2 in the first place?

Novote.

item.title: Image Source
item.content:encoded:
The image used for SCP-3389 is titled "Creepiness in the Water" by Malcolm Moore. I pulled it from the Visual Records Wiki.

Original Source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/malcolmm/3283579999/
Creator: Malcolm Moore
